steps.title: Let's build a Pin on Image slide!
steps.description: A Pin on Image slide lets participants tap directly on a custom image to mark their answer — perfect for maps, diagrams, and spatial feedback. We'll build one together in under a minute. Click Next to begin.

steps.title: Create a new presentation
steps.description: Click 'New Presentation' to start a fresh deck.

steps.title: Pick the Pin on Image slide type
steps.description: Select 'Pin on Image' from the slide type picker. You'll find it in the Unscored section — it's the one that turns any image into an interactive pin-dropping activity.

steps.title: Write your question
steps.description: Type a spatial prompt for your audience — something that tells them exactly where to look and what to mark.

action.value: Where on this map is your hometown?
action.hint: Type your question, then click away

steps.title: Open the image library
steps.description: Click the image area to open the image library and choose a photo for your slide.

steps.title: Browse the image library
steps.description: Your image library is open. Switch to Stock Photos to browse millions of free, searchable images.

steps.title: Search, pick, and save your image
steps.description: Click 'Stock Photos', type 'world map' in the search box, pick the first result, then hit Save to add it to your slide.

steps.title: Rehearse with Preview
steps.description: Click Preview to see exactly how your slide will look to participants — no audience needed.

steps.title: This is your audience's phone
steps.description: The participant screen on the right shows your image with the question. Your audience opens this on their phone and taps directly on the image to drop a pin — wherever they think the answer is.

steps.title: Pins appear live on the big screen
steps.description: On the presenter screen, every pin lands in real time as responses come in. You see exactly where your audience marked their answers — overlaid on your image.

steps.title: Head back to the editor
steps.description: Done previewing? Click the back arrow to return to the editor.

action.hint: Click the back arrow to continue

steps.title: Ready to go live!
steps.description: That's all it takes — a question, a stock photo or your own image, and you're set. Hit 'Present' when it's showtime and your audience joins on their phones to pin their answers in real time.
